wellness.title: Arrive & Breathe
wellness.body: You've made it — well done. Before we head to the trail, take 3 slow breaths together. In for 4 counts, hold for 4, out for 6. Let the morning's rush settle. Today is about presence, not pace.

activity.title: Set an Intention
activity.body: As a team, each person shares one word for how they want to feel by the end of the walk. No wrong answers.

wellness.title: One Rock at a Time
wellness.body: This old quarry was cleared stone by stone. When everything feels like too much, it helps to do the same — pick one small thing and begin. Breathe, feel your feet on the ground, and let the pile shrink one rock at a time.

activity.title: Name the Boulder
activity.body: As a team, each person names one 'big rock' on their mind this week, then one small first step toward it. Say it out loud — a shared load is lighter.

wellness.title: Still Water
wellness.body: The quarry's roar gave way to this quiet wetland — things only settle once you step away from the noise. Do that now: leave the trail's push for a minute, pause at the water's edge, soften your gaze, and let your breathing slow. A real break isn't time lost — it's what makes the rest of the day work.

activity.title: Spot the Life
activity.body: Stand quietly for a minute and watch the water. As a team, count how many living things you can find — birds, fish, dragonflies. Nature rewards patience.

wellness.title: The Hidden Deck
wellness.body: You found it. Colugos glide between these trees at dusk, and durians drop without warning below. Reaching this secret earns you the Durian Dodger title. You left the obvious path and found something worth it — take the view in.

activity.title: Glide Like a Colugo
activity.body: A colugo travels by letting go and trusting the glide. Name one thing you've been gripping too tightly at work — then, just for this moment, let it go and enjoy the view.

wellness.title: Leave It at the Gate
wellness.body: This gate is where the trail — and your own time — begins. Step through and let the outside world stay outside: silence the buzzing, drop your shoulders, unclench your jaw, and let your pace slow to match the forest. Off is off — that boundary is how the calm actually reaches you. The rest of the trail opens up from here.

activity.title: First Steps Together
activity.body: Head off as a team — no one walks alone. For the first minute, walk in silence and simply notice your breathing and the sounds around you.

wellness.title: Get Up and Play
wellness.body: Your body wasn't built to sit still for hours — and your focus wasn't either. There's a playground right here, so use it: swing, stretch, climb, shake the stiffness out. A minute of moving resets you more than pushing through ever will.

wellness.title: Find Your Rhythm
wellness.body: A long swing is just gentle, rocking motion — the same rhythm that settles a restless mind. Hop on, let your shoulders drop, and let the back-and-forth slow your breathing for a minute.

activity.title: The Long Swing
activity.body: There's a long swing here — take turns and enjoy the ride. Go one at a time, keep a firm grip, and watch the space around you. Don't push it too high — the aim is a big grin, not a twisted ankle.

wellness.title: Widen Your View
wellness.body: Up close, a setback fills the whole frame. Step back to a wide view like this and it shrinks to what it really is — one hard moment, not the whole story. Soften your focus, take in the distance, and let that same wide lens fall on whatever's been weighing on you.

wellness.title: Arrive & Reflect
wellness.body: You've closed the loop — and you didn't do it alone. Notice how your body and mind feel now versus when you started, and who was around you for it. The easiest walks are the shared ones; the same is true back at your desk. Carry that — and a little of this calm — back with you.

activity.title: Final Group Photo
activity.body: Gather the whole team for a group photo to mark the end of the walk — smiles mandatory.
